title: Stopping Password-Guessing Attacks
description:
## Defending Against Credential Stuffing

Your logs show repeated failed login attempts against many accounts — a password-guessing / credential-stuffing attack. You want Keycloak to **automatically slow down or stop** these attempts. What should you enable?

options.text: **Brute force detection** in Realm Settings → Security Defenses, which temporarily locks an account after repeated failed logins.
Just a stronger **password policy**; complexity rules alone stop guessing attacks.
Rely solely on a **WAF** in front of Keycloak; the IdP itself can't help.
Increase the **access-token lifespan** so attackers re-authenticate less often.

explanation: Keycloak has built-in **brute force detection**, configured under **Realm Settings → Security Defenses**. After a configurable number of failed attempts it can **temporarily lock** the account (with options for permanent lockout, wait increments, and quick-login checks), directly throttling guessing attacks. Option **b** helps but doesn't *stop* automated guessing — strong passwords can still be attacked at volume, and reused passwords from breaches bypass complexity entirely. Option **c** is wrong because the IdP *can* and should defend itself; a WAF is complementary, not a replacement, and can't see per-account failure counts. Option **d** is unrelated — token lifespan has nothing to do with login-attempt throttling.

hints.title: Where to look
hints.body: It's a realm-level toggle under Security Defenses. Tune the failure threshold and lockout duration to balance security against locking out legitimate users.
